creator: junchoon@dec.sakura.ne.jp
text:
(In reply to Boris Korzun from comment #9)
I should have asked at the first place, but...

*Are you loading nvidia-modeset.ko, or nvidia.ko?
 The former attempts to auto-load the latter, too. But the latter does not.

If the latter, the X11 driver wouldn't work correctly, but as the driver seems to be loaded and at least partially initialized according to your log, it would be unlikely.


*How are you loading driver kernel module(s)?
  Via kld_list variable in your /etc/rc.conf?
  Or via your /boot/loader.conf?

If the former, this could be the GPU-specific issue that nvidia needs to fix in their blob (non onen-sourced) part.

If the latter, try to switch to the former.
The staging memory area, that loader reads kernel and all existing modules listed in the /boot/loader.conf, is limited in size and huge modules like nvidia GPU driver could overflow. In these cases, loader truncates the module on loading not to actually overflown.

This can cause any kind of errors (bus error, bad syscall, sigsegv,...) when anywhere in the truncated area are somehow accessed. The acutal error may vary by how the access is done.

As 470 series of nvidia driver already are "unified drivers" that support mutiple GPUs in single binary, if actually required parts are in the sanely-loaded area, it would work. But if in the truncated area, it wouldn't. This can match your breakage, in my wild guess.

And as I'm not a nvidia insider, I'm not at the position that can know actual code layouts etc.
